market_news.content: This article examines whether the current stock market sell-off presents a good buying opportunity for the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) ETF, which tracks the Nasdaq-100. It highlights that the Nasdaq-100, heavily weighted in technology stocks, has historically delivered strong returns despite volatility and bear markets. The author concludes that based on past performance and the continued growth of sectors like AI, investing in QQQ during downturns has proven beneficial.

items.title: 85% test AI agents, but only 5% use them; Cisco adds security
items.content: Cisco (NASDAQ: CSCO) has introduced a new suite of security innovations designed to secure the emerging "agentic AI" workforce, where software agents perform actions rather than just answer questions. While a recent Cisco survey shows 85% of enterprises are experimenting with AI agents, only 5% have moved them into production, highlighting a gap in trust and security. Cisco's new offerings include Zero Trust Access for AI agents, the AI Defense: Explorer Edition for self-service red teaming, the

items.title: BlackRock CEO Fink backs staying invested amid volatility, flags AI shift
items.content: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink is advising investors to remain in the market despite current volatility, citing geopolitical conflicts, economic shifts, and the transformative impact of artificial intelligence. He highlighted AI's role in reshaping industries and creating significant economic value, emphasizing that staying invested over the long term has historically outweighed attempts at market timing. items.title: NVIDIA and Emerald AI Join Leading Energy Companies to Pioneer Flexible AI Factories as Grid Assets
items.content: NVIDIA and Emerald AI announced a collaboration with major energy companies such as AES, Constellation, and NextEra Energy to establish "flexible AI factories." These AI factories will leverage NVIDIA's Vera Rubin DSX AI Factory reference design and DSX Flex software, along with Emerald AI’s Conductor platform, to connect to the grid faster, generate AI tokens, and operate as flexible energy assets supporting grid reliability. items.title: F5 Partners NVIDIA To Scale AI Inference Platforms
items.content: F5 has expanded its collaboration with NVIDIA to enhance AI inference platforms by integrating F5 BIG-IP Next for Kubernetes with NVIDIA BlueField-3 DPUs. This partnership aims to improve token throughput, reduce latency, and enable secure multi-tenant AI platforms at scale. items.content: Procyon Advisors LLC significantly increased its stake in Micron Technology by 392.7%, acquiring an additional 5,101 shares to hold a total of 6,400 shares valued at $1.83 million. This increase follows Micron's strong quarterly results, driven by AI memory demand, which exceeded revenue and EPS expectations. Despite positive analyst ratings and increased dividends, the stock has experienced some profit-taking and investor concern over rising capital expenditure plans.

items.title: Qualcomm Inc Stock Bolstered by $20 Billion Buyback Amid Semiconductor Volatility
items.content: Qualcomm Inc. has announced a significant $20 billion share buyback program and a 3.4% dividend hike to $0.89 per share to counteract a 24% stock decline in early 2026. These strategic financial moves, supported by strong Q1 2026 results and diversification into AI, automotive, and IoT segments, aim to stabilize the stock and reward shareholders. The company views its stock as undervalued and positions itself for growth despite semiconductor market volatility and geopolitical risks.

items.title: Upstage Talks Put AMD MI355 In Focus For Asian AI Growth
items.content: South Korean AI startup Upstage is in advanced talks to purchase 10,000 AMD MI355 AI accelerators, indicating growing demand for AMD's offerings beyond Nvidia in the Asian AI market. This potential deal would bolster AMD's presence in South Korea and serve as an important reference point for future AI build-outs in the region. For investors, it highlights AMD's progress in becoming a system-level AI supplier and the diversification of its AI revenue base, though execution risks for new products

items.title: F5 and NVIDIA expand integration to improve AI inference efficiency
items.content: F5 and NVIDIA have deepened their technical integration, combining F5's BIG-IP Next for Kubernetes with NVIDIA's BlueField-3 DPUs to enhance AI inference efficiency. This collaboration aims to offload networking and traffic management tasks from GPUs, allowing them to focus solely on AI model execution. The solution also supports multi-tenant GPU environments and integrates with NVIDIA's DOCA Platform Framework.

items.title: NVIDIA Corporation $NVDA Shares Purchased by Eagle Wealth Strategies LLC
items.content: Eagle Wealth Strategies LLC increased its stake in NVIDIA Corporation by 25.8% in Q4, holding 14,677 shares valued at $2.74 million. This move comes amidst NVIDIA's strong financial performance driven by AI chip demand, including a major deal with Amazon Web Services. However, the article also notes recent insider selling and regulatory scrutiny, while market analysts largely maintain a "Buy" rating for NVDA with an average target price of $275.40.

items.title: SK Telecom is speeding up its transition to an artificial intelligence (AI) company by promoting its..
items.content: SK Telecom is accelerating its transformation into an AI company by implementing an "AI native" strategy. This involves redesigning its entire communication service infrastructure with AI at the core, establishing itself as a major player in the domestic AI industry through significant investments in AI infrastructure, and expanding its own foundational AI models. The company aims to provide ultra-personalized services, enhance information protection, and develop AI-driven network operations.

items.title: Activist Elliott takes multibillion-dollar stake in Synopsys, source says
items.content: Activist investor Elliott Investment Management has acquired a multibillion-dollar stake in Synopsys, an electronic design automation company, aiming to push for increased sales and improved margins. Synopsys, valued at over $80 billion, is a crucial supplier of software for chip design, and Elliott believes there's a strong opportunity for its financial performance to better reflect its value, especially with AI driving increased chip complexity. The hedge fund plans to engage with Synopsys to

items.title: Is Marvell’s New AI Connectivity Push And Dividend Policy Altering The Investment Case For MRVL?
items.content: Marvell Technology, Inc. recently expanded its AI data center connectivity lineup with new PCIe 6.0 and CXL 3.0 switches and declared a quarterly dividend. While these advancements strengthen Marvell's position in high-bandwidth, low-latency AI infrastructure, the company's revenue remains highly sensitive to a few large cloud and AI customers. Investors need to weigh the compelling AI story against the risks of concentrated hyperscaler spending and potential capex slowdowns.

items.title: Akamai Launches 'AI Grid Intelligent Orchestration,' Claims First Commercial Global AI Grid
items.content: Akamai Technologies has launched an "AI Grid Intelligent Orchestration" built on Nvidia's AI Grid Reference Design, claiming it's the first commercial global AI grid. This initiative transforms Akamai's content delivery network (CDN) into an AI edge inference network, utilizing over 4,400 global edge locations to process AI workloads closer to end-users. The company aims to reduce latency and costs for enterprise AI, leveraging Nvidia AI infrastructure and thousands of Nvidia RTX Pro 6000 Blackw

items.content: The Invesco S&P 500 Top 50 ETF (XLG) has declared a reduced quarterly dividend of $0.0939 per share, down from $0.0968, reflecting a shift in cash allocation by its major technology holdings. Invesco has also removed XLG from its model portfolios to mitigate concentration risk, given the ETF's heavy weighting towards top tech companies like Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft. items.title: Tech Sector ETFs Navigate a Turbulent Market Landscape
items.content: The technology sector has experienced significant volatility in 2026, with the iShares Expanded Tech Sector ETF declining 4.39% due to geopolitical tensions and interest rate uncertainties. Despite this, companies focused on AI infrastructure are seen as key growth drivers. Investors are advised to consider fee structures, diversification strategies, and the impact of central bank policies on the sector.

items.content: Activist investor Elliott Investment Management has reportedly acquired a multibillion-dollar stake in Synopsys. Elliott plans to engage with Synopsys to enhance its financial performance, particularly by increasing revenue and improving margins from its software and services, which it believes can be more aligned with its closest rival, Cadence Design Systems. items.title: Automotive News explores repair costs of megacasting as Ford plans use in future EV
items.content: Ford plans to incorporate megacasting into its future mid-sized electric pickup trucks aiming to lower both manufacturing and repair costs. While initial concerns about higher repair expenses due to large, single-piece components were raised, research from Thatcham Research on the Tesla Model Y suggests that properly implemented megacasting can lead to lower repair costs and improved sustainability. items.content: Stock futures were down on Monday following President Donald Trump's threat to "hit and obliterate" Iranian power plants if the Strait of Hormuz was not reopened within 48 hours. This article lists several stock market movers for the day, including Tesla, SMCI, Synopsys, Lumentum, NEM, and FCX against the backdrop of this geopolitical tension.

This meeting highlights ongoing engagement between Chinese officials and major international corporations. items.title: With the MacBook Neo, Apple Made the Perfect AI Computer
items.content: Apple's new MacBook Neo, priced under $600, is being highlighted as an ideal AI computer despite its compromises. The article argues that because most AI computing happens in the cloud, the Neo's A18 Pro chip, 8GB memory, and SSD are perfectly adequate for running AI tools like ChatGPT or Gemini. This makes the affordable MacBook Neo a highly accessible AI-first computer, redefining what specifications are crucial for modern computing.

items.title: Was Warren Buffett’s Coca-Cola Investment a Mistake?
items.content: Warren Buffett's investment in Coca-Cola, initiated in 1988, has yielded superior total returns compared to PepsiCo over various timeframes, demonstrating it was not a mistake. Coca-Cola's status as a Dividend King with over a century of uninterrupted payouts contributed to its outperformance. The article highlights that while entry dates can affect comparative returns, Buffett's timely decision to invest in Coca-Cola was a strategic move that reflects patience and effective brand investing.

To mitigate risks, Gartner advises security leaders to engage early in AI application projects, plan resources, and implement adequate security controls. items.title: Biden officials who backed Microsoft software breached by China now work for Microsoft
items.content: Two former high-level Biden administration officials, Melinda Rogers and Lisa Monaco, who were involved in the Department of Justice's evaluation and adoption of Microsoft's GCC cloud services, have now taken senior positions at Microsoft. This comes after Chinese hackers breached the GCC system, and despite federal auditors raising security concerns and potential conflicts of interest regarding Microsoft's ability to prove the security of its cloud products. The article highlights the officials

items.title: Should You Buy the Invesco QQQ ETF During the Stock Market Sell-Off? History Offers a Clear Answer.
items.content: This article examines whether the current stock market sell-off presents a good buying opportunity for the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) ETF, which tracks the Nasdaq-100. It highlights that the Nasdaq-100, heavily weighted in technology stocks, has historically delivered strong returns despite volatility and bear markets. The author concludes that based on past performance and the continued growth of sectors like AI, investing in QQQ during downturns has proven beneficial.

items.title: Should You Buy the Invesco QQQ ETF During the Stock Market Sell-Off? History Offers a Clear Answer.
items.content: The article discusses the Invesco QQQ ETF, which tracks the Nasdaq-100 index, composed of the 100 most valuable nonfinancial companies on the Nasdaq, with a significant allocation to technology stocks. Despite recent volatility and the Nasdaq-100 being down almost 7% from its peak, history suggests that investing in the QQQ ETF, even during sell-offs, tends to be beneficial in the long term. items.title: Holos Integrated Wealth LLC Invests $774,000 in Microsoft Corporation $MSFT
items.content: Holos Integrated Wealth LLC recently acquired a new stake of 1,637 shares in Microsoft Corporation, valued at approximately $774,000. Institutional investors now own about 71.13% of MSFT, despite recent price target trims by analysts, who maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus due to AI-driven Azure demand and the OpenAI partnership. Insider activity shows EVP Kathleen Hogan selling shares while Director John W. Stanton bought shares, and Microsoft announced a quarterly dividend of $0.91 per share.

items.title: Doliver Advisors LP Has $3.30 Million Holdings in Microsoft Corporation $MSFT
items.content: Doliver Advisors LP increased its stake in Microsoft Corporation by 30.8% in the fourth quarter, bringing its total holdings to 6,815 shares valued at $3.30 million. This makes Microsoft the 16th largest holding for the institutional investor. The article also details other institutional investor activity, Microsoft's stock performance, recent earnings, announced dividends, analyst ratings, and insider transactions.

items.title: Microsoft Corporation $MSFT Stock Position Lowered by Argent Capital Management LLC
items.content: Argent Capital Management LLC reduced its stake in Microsoft Corporation by 2.8% in Q4, selling 13,459 shares but still holding 472,969 shares valued at $228.74 million. Despite insider selling by an EVP, a director acquired shares, and analysts largely maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with a target price of $591.87 following strong quarterly results. items.content: Anderson Hoagland & Co. reduced its stake in Microsoft Corporation by 2.0% in the fourth quarter, selling 2,182 shares but still holding 106,355 shares valued at $51.435 million, making it their second-largest position. Microsoft reported strong quarterly earnings, beating estimates with $4.14 EPS and $81.27 billion in revenue, and declared a $0.91 quarterly dividend. items.title: Navigating Tech Volatility: The Income-Focused Approach of the Global X QRMI ETF ()
items.content: The Global X NASDAQ 100® Risk Managed Income ETF (QRMI) is designed for investors seeking exposure to the technology sector while mitigating volatility and generating income. It achieves this by holding Nasdaq 100 stocks and employing a net-credit options collar strategy, involving the purchase of put options for downside protection and the sale of call options for income generation. The fund offers monthly distributions, a notable yield of 12.03% as of March 2026, and a total expense ratio of 0

items.title: Invesco's S&P 500 Top 50 ETF Announces Lower Quarterly Payout
items.content: The Invesco S&P 500 Top 50 ETF (XLG) has declared a reduced quarterly dividend of $0.0939 per share, down from $0.0968, reflecting a shift in cash allocation by its major technology holdings. Invesco has also removed XLG from its model portfolios to mitigate concentration risk, given the ETF's heavy weighting towards top tech companies like Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft. The fund is currently in a consolidation phase, and investors are watching for upcoming rebalancing to see if its tech-heavy pr

items.title: Tech Sector ETFs Navigate a Turbulent Market Landscape
items.content: The technology sector has experienced significant volatility in 2026, with the iShares Expanded Tech Sector ETF declining 4.39% due to geopolitical tensions and interest rate uncertainties. Despite this, companies focused on AI infrastructure are seen as key growth drivers. Investors are advised to consider fee structures, diversification strategies, and the impact of central bank policies on the sector.

Alphabet (GOOGL) Stock Drops 13% From All-Time High — Time to Buy the Dip?
items.content: Alphabet (GOOGL) stock has fallen 13% from its all-time high, trading below $300 amidst a global market sell-off driven by Middle East tensions. Despite strong Q4 2025 earnings and revenue exceeding $400 billion for the first time, investor sentiment is impacted by the company's substantial 2026 capital expenditure plans for AI infrastructure. Analysts maintain a "Strong Buy" consensus with an average 12-month price target of $351.82, suggesting potential for significant upside.

items.title: NextEra gets federal approval to build 10GW of natural gas power in Texas and Pennsylvania for data centers
items.content: NextEra Energy has received federal approval to develop 10GW of natural gas power generation facilities in Texas and Pennsylvania to meet the growing demand from data centers. This development is linked to Japan's recent $550 billion investment commitment to the US market, with the projects to be jointly owned by both countries.
